---
title: "Length Stratify boarfish data"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Read data
boarfish <- read.csv("/Users/jessicawestworth/Desktop/caprosaper_jessicawestworth.csv")

bin_width <- 1

length_age_ls <- boarfish %>%
    #Create length classes
  mutate(length = cut(
    TL_cm,
    breaks = seq(floor(min(TL_cm)), ceiling(max(TL_cm)), by = bin_width),
    right = FALSE
  )) %>%
  
    #Convert length class to lower bound
  mutate(length = as.numeric(sub("\\[(\\d+),.*", "\\1", length))) %>%
  
    #Split CAPTURE_DATE into day/month/year
  separate(CAPTURE_DATE, into = c("day", "month", "year"), sep = "-", convert = TRUE) %>%
    
    # Add Quarter
  mutate(Quarter = case_when(
    month %in% 1:3 ~ 1,
    month %in% 4:6 ~ 2,
    month %in% 7:9 ~ 3,
    month %in% 10:12 ~ 4
  )) %>%
    
    # Count number of fish per combination (long format)
  count(day, month, year, Quarter, length, AGE_yrs, name = "N") %>%
    
    # Expand counts to individual rows for LS sampling
  uncount(N) %>%
    
    # Length-stratified subsample: up to 10 fish per length class per date
  group_by(day, month, year, length) %>%
  slice_sample(n = 10, replace = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
    
    # Recount to get LS table: N per age
  count(day, month, year, Quarter, length, AGE_yrs, name = "N") %>%
  ungroup()
```
